folio.label: 12v
folio.l: Here in this place , sittynge´ by this post´
Whan æ we comen a geyn , fro Caunterbury
And for to make ©ow , the more mery
I wyl my self goodly , with yow ryde
Rigút at myn owen cost , and be ©oure guyde
And who so wyl , my Iuggement with sey
Shal paie al that we spende , by the wey
And if ©e wouchesaue , æat it be so
Telle me a non , w oute wordes mo
And I wyl erly , shape me therfore
This æingg´ was gunted , and oure othes swore
With ful glad hert , and prayed hi~ also
That he wold vouchesaue , so to do
And that he wolde be , oure gouänour
And of oure tales , Iugge , and reportour
And sette a soper , at a certein prise
And we wyl reuled be , at his deuyse
In heygh & lowe , and thus by one assent
We be acorded , to his Iuggement´
And ther vp on , the wyn was fet a non~
We dronkyn & to rest , wenten ichon~
With outen eny lengere , tariyng´
¢ A morwe , whan æe day gan spryng´
Vp ros oure Ost , and was oure aller Cok´
And gadered vs to gedir , on a flok´
And forth we redyn , a litel pas
Vn to the watäyng´ , of Seynt Thomas
And there oure Ost , bygan his hors arest´
And seide lordes , herkeneth if ©ow list´
ñe wete oure forward , if ©e it recor¶
If euesong´ , and morwesong´ accor¶
Late se now , who shal telle æe first tale
As euäe mote I drynke , wyn or ale
Who so be rebel , to my Iuggement´
Shal paie for al æ is , by the weye I spent´
Now draweth cutte , er we ferthere twynne
For he æ hath the shortest , shal begynne
¢ Sire knygút q¶ he , my maister & my lor¶
Now draweth Cutte , for this is myn accor¶
Cometh nere quod he , my lady Prioresse
And ©e sire clerk , late be ©oure shamefastnesse
